title: Indulgent Smoked Korean Flour Bao
description: Herbal, crisp, and indulgent Korean dish made with all-purpose flour, robata-grill, and a hint of Korean spices.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine warm water, yeast, and 1 teaspoon of sugar. Let it sit for 5 minutes until yeast is activated.
Add flour, salt, and 1 tablespoon of sesame oil to the bowl. Mix until dough forms.
Knead dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Divide dough into 4 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Brush the top of each dough with sesame oil and sprinkle with Korean chili flakes, ground ginger, and green onions.
Place each dough on the robata-grill and cook for 2-3 minutes until it starts to puff up.
Flip the dough over and cook for another 2-3 minutes until it's golden brown.
Slice the smoked tofu into thin strips and place it on top of the dough.
Brush the top with roasted garlic and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Serve the Indulgent Smoked Korean Flour Bao immediately and enjoy.
